client_updates.highlight: Grandine: FCR-specific tests passing; PR pending review from Thomas
Lodestar: FCR shipped in v1.44.0 (experimental flag); epoch-boundary reset fix deployed Friday, no resets since
Lighthouse: regression from prior call resolved; fix in progress, mainnet runs show no resets
Teku: production-ready FCR implementation underway; Prysm blocked on human review

fcr_behavior_and_gloas.highlight: Decision: FCR should not trigger during node sync; only activate once fully caught up with head
FCR on Gloas: confirmed payload from two slots prior (not one) due to Gloas timing change
Some clients (e.g., Nimbus) enable FCR by default; others require a feature flag
Barnabas: FCR should be tested on Gloas devnet 7; Dora visualization of FCR event stream planned

spec_and_testing.highlight: FCR manual test suite complete; all planned cases covered (PR merged)
Mainnet-config test vector generation took ~3 hours per test; minimal preset deemed sufficient
FCR metrics PR merged; teams encouraged to implement across all clients

research_updates.highlight: Proof review complete; pseudocode differences from Python spec to be addressed in paper appendix — last blocker before publication
Late-block recovery optimization (~1–1.5% more single-slot confirmations, prototyped in Teku) under consideration; proof work costly and may conflict with Casper research

action_items.action: Nazar to share epoch-boundary reset analysis and fix details in FCR Telegram group
Mikhail to share Teku late-block optimization code reference in FCR Telegram group

decisions.decision: FCR must not trigger during node sync; activation deferred until node is fully synced with head (agreed with Nimbus and Lodestar)

targets.target: Cross-client FCR compatibility testing to be coordinated on next FCR breakout call (~2 weeks)
FCR paper publication pending appendix clarifying pseudocode vs. Python spec differences
